ambiguities.description: What entities, relationships, and boundaries define this system?
How do users and processes interact with the system over time?
What rules, constraints, and compliance requirements apply?
What external systems, integrations, and deployment constraints exist?
What defines MVP, done, and out-of-scope for this construction?
Property, listing, lease, and tenant ontology (units, buildings, portfolios)
Listing workflow (creation, marketing, inquiry, showing, application)
Applications and screening workflow (criteria, fees, background, approval)
Lease lifecycle (draft, sign, move-in, renew, terminate, move-out)
Payments and deposits model (rent, deposit, fees, late charges, methods)
Maintenance requests and work order tracking
Broker, agent, owner, and property manager roles
Compliance and jurisdiction (local landlord-tenant law, fair housing, disclosures)
Integrations with MLS, accounting, or payment processors
MVP transaction and operations boundary (listings only, leases only, full lifecycle)

questions.question: What does success look like for this system?
Who are the primary users and what is the organization size?
What is the timeline and what constraints (budget, compliance, team) apply?
Is the focus residential, commercial, or mixed?
What is the primary business model? (sales, rentals, property management, mixed)
What roles exist? (owner, agent, property manager, tenant, applicant)
Are lease execution and payment collection in scope?
What compliance or jurisdiction constraints apply?
What payment methods must be supported?
Are MLS or accounting integrations required?

assumptions.assumption: Properties are discrete addressable units with known owners or managers
Tenants or buyers are identified individuals with contact information
Sales vs rentals is not assumed without explicit policy
Jurisdiction-specific compliance must be explicitly declared

suggested_closures.decision: Real estate construction will explicitly model property, lease, and tenant entities with a chosen residential/commercial and sales/rentals focus.
suggested_closures.rationale: These choices determine the entire ontology, workflow, and compliance surface.

seed_tasks.title: Define property and unit ontology
seed_tasks.transformation: Document how properties, units, and portfolios are structured.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Entity model is documented with relationships.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: A developer can implement property lookup and unit assignment from the document.

seed_tasks.title: Define lease lifecycle and states
seed_tasks.transformation: Document lease states from application through termination.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: Any lease can be classified by state and next permissible transition.

seed_tasks.title: Define payment and deposit model
seed_tasks.transformation: Document rent, deposit, fee structures, and payment methods.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Payment model is documented with fee schedule.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: A tenant can understand what they owe and how to pay.

seed_tasks.title: Define compliance and jurisdiction framework
seed_tasks.transformation: Document applicable laws, disclosures, and fair housing requirements.
seed_tasks.evidence_requirement: Compliance checklist is documented by jurisdiction.
seed_tasks.review_predicate: All required disclosures are listed and mapped to lease events.

residuals.description: System scope and success criteria are not yet defined by the principal.
Constraints, compliance requirements, and non-goals have not been documented.
Property and unit ontology is not defined.
Lease lifecycle model is not defined.
Payment and deposit model is undefined.
Compliance and jurisdiction requirements are not documented.
